title: Maundy Thursday
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
Sermon Date: April 1, 2021 | Preacher: Richard Lawson

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

April 1, 2021 was Maundy Thursday, the day before Good Friday in the Christian calendar, falling within Holy Week of the Easter season. The RCL readings for Maundy Thursday included Exodus 12:1-14 (the Passover account), 1 Corinthians 11:23-26 (Paul's account of the Last Supper), and John 13:1-17, 31b-35 (Jesus washing the disciples' feet). This date marked the beginning of the Triduum, the three holiest days in the Christian liturgical year, emphasizing themes of sacrifice, servanthood, and covenant renewal.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the early months of the tenure of Presiding Bishop Sean Rowe, who had been elected in July 2021 (so Richard Lawson would have been serving under outgoing Presiding Bishop Michael Curry at this time). The Diocese of Colorado, which oversees Saint John's Cathedral, was led by Bishop Kym Harris at this time. No major denominational controversies or General Convention actions were immediately pending, as the next General Convention would occur in July 2021.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver in late March 2021 was experiencing early spring weather with the region recovering from winter storms; Colorado had been dealing with ongoing pandemic-related capacity restrictions on religious gatherings, though vaccination rates were beginning to increase. The Colorado State Capitol and downtown Denver area, where the Cathedral is located, were resuming more in-person activities as COVID-19 case numbers began declining. Local news was focused on the April 6 mayoral election in Denver, with candidates campaigning actively in the final week before the primary.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The United States was in the midst of its vaccination rollout, with President Biden announcing that all Americans would be eligible for COVID-19 vaccines by May 1, 2021. The trial of Derek Chauvin for the murder of George Floyd (from May 2020) was in its final week of testimony before the jury verdict on April 20, 2021, dominating national news coverage. The U.S. southern border was experiencing a significant surge in migrant arrivals, a developing crisis that would intensify throughout spring 2021, generating political debate about immigration policy.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The European Union was experiencing a third wave of COVID-19 infections with new lockdown measures being implemented across multiple countries. India was beginning to experience the devastating second wave of COVID-19 that would peak in May 2021 with record case numbers and oxygen shortages. Myanmar remained in crisis following the February 2021 military coup, with ongoing protests and civil disobedience against the junta.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture in early April 2021 occupied a threshold moment: vaccination hope was rising but pandemic fatigue was profound, with many people beginning to envision a return to normal while still under significant restrictions. There was cautious optimism mixed with exhaustion after a year of isolation; religious communities were particularly attuned to the significance of in-person worship returning. The nation was also deeply polarized and mourning—waiting for the Chauvin verdict while processing ongoing racial justice reckoning—making the Maundy Thursday themes of servant love and washing one's enemy's feet particularly resonant and challenging for congregations.
